chunks:
QUIZ OUTLINE — CIRCULAR MOTION || Key concepts for SPM-level assessment
1. CENTRIPETAL FORCE || Inward force keeping an object on a circular path.
2. ANGULAR VELOCITY || Rate of change of angle, in rad/s.
SUGGESTED FOCUS || Emphasize the relationship between linear and angular quantities.

request.subject: Physics
request.outline:
1. CENTRIPETAL FORCE || Inward force.
2. ANGULAR VELOCITY || Rate of change of angle.

canned.text: [{"question_stem":"Which force is required to keep an object moving in a circle?","options":{"A":"Centrifugal force","B":"Centripetal force","C":"Frictional force","D":"Normal force"},"correct_answer":"B","detailed_explanation":"**Centripetal force** points toward the centre of the circle."},{"question_stem":"What are the SI units of angular velocity?","options":{"A":"m/s","B":"m/s²","C":"rad/s","D":"Hz"},"correct_answer":"C","detailed_explanation":"Angular velocity is measured in **rad/s**."},{"question_stem":"For uniform circular motion, what direction is the acceleration?","options":{"A":"Tangent to the path","B":"Outward radial","C":"Inward radial","D":"Zero"},"correct_answer":"C","detailed_explanation":"Acceleration points toward the centre."},{"question_stem":"Doubling the speed of circular motion at fixed radius changes centripetal force by what factor?","options":{"A":"×1","B":"×2","C":"×4","D":"×0.5"},"correct_answer":"C","detailed_explanation":"F=mv²/r so doubling v multiplies F by **four**."},{"question_stem":"Period T relates to angular velocity ω as:","options":{"A":"T=ω/2π","B":"T=2π/ω","C":"T=2πω","D":"T=π/ω"},"correct_answer":"B","detailed_explanation":"By definition T=**2π/ω**."}]

request.subject: Biology
request.outline:
1. DOMINANT ALLELE || Expressed when present.
2. PUNNETT SQUARE || Cross diagram.

canned.text: [{"question_stem":"In a heterozygous Tt individual, which allele is expressed?","options":{"A":"T","B":"t","C":"Both equally","D":"Neither"},"correct_answer":"A","detailed_explanation":"The **dominant** allele T masks the recessive allele t."},{"question_stem":"What does a Punnett square show?","options":{"A":"DNA sequence","B":"Possible genotype combinations","C":"Protein structure","D":"Cell cycle"},"correct_answer":"B","detailed_explanation":"A Punnett square predicts **genotype** combinations from a cross."},{"question_stem":"Cross Tt × Tt yields what phenotype ratio?","options":{"A":"1:1","B":"3:1","C":"9:3:3:1","D":"1:2:1"},"correct_answer":"B","detailed_explanation":"Phenotype ratio is **3:1** dominant:recessive."}]

[{"question_stem":"What is the SI unit of velocity?","options":{"A":"m","B":"m/s","C":"m/s²","D":"N"},"correct_answer":"B","detailed_explanation":"Velocity is measured in **m/s**."},{"question_stem":"Speed is a:","options":{"A":"Vector","B":"Scalar","C":"Tensor","D":"Matrix"},"correct_answer":"B","detailed_explanation":"Speed is a **scalar** quantity."},{"question_stem":"Acceleration is the rate of change of:","options":{"A":"Position","B":"Velocity","C":"Mass","D":"Time"},"correct_answer":"B","detailed_explanation":"a = dv/dt."}]
